title: runtime external tool-operator Tool command task cards
purpose: I4.2 defines runtime external tool operator tool command task cards for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I4.2` only; siblings under `I4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: When an implement task card declares a Tool command block, the conductor spawns a tool-operator shell worker instead of a phase implement worker, binding execution to the literal CLI on the card.
The tool-operator worker runs pack-declared commands—Blender batch exports, Unreal commandlets, custom CLIs—and may write outputs only to paths explicitly declared on the task card.
tool-operator skill bounds the command surface and filesystem writes; product source edits remain the domain of normal implement workers, not shell tool runs.
On completion, stdout/stderr and artifact paths attach to evidence/ and route through [B2.4](B2.4-verifier-tool-operator-evidence.md) before last_verify can pass and pursuit advances.
Tool failures, missing outputs, or ad-hoc invocations without a bound task card fail closed with verify escalation—pursuit turns stop rather than improvising undocumented tooling.

**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: I4.2 defines runtime external tool operator tool command task cards for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system when an implement task card declares a tool command block, the conductor spawns a tool-operator shell worker instead of a phase implement worker, binding execution to the literal cli on the card, then the tool-operator worker runs pack-declared commands—blender batch exports, unreal commandlets, custom clis—and may write outputs only to paths explicitly declared on the task card.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

Tool-operator shell workers execute literal Tool command blocks from task cards—Blender batch exports, Unreal commandlet runs, custom CLIs—writing outputs only to declared paths. tool-operator skill bounds writes; product source edits happen only via normal implement workers.

Tool failures attach logs to evidence/ and trigger verify escalation ([B2.4](B2.4-verifier-tool-operator-evidence.md)). Pack authors declare tool patterns in task card templates; ad-hoc tool invocation without card binding is forbidden during evidence-gated implement.
